text:
# Page 1^page=1
Household Financial Policy This document formalizes the operational structure for the 1745 S Elliot household bill management system. The primary goal is to maintain clear responsibility and predictable contributions while minimizing administrative friction and ADHD-related stress. Core Infrastructure The system utilizes a two-tier approach for tracking and physical fund management: ● Tracking Engine: TBD ( Splitwise is an option) . ● Funding Engine ( Cash App Pools) : Cody and Zai will send funds to the pool Mom should create or directly to Mom if no pool exists. Mom must update Cody and Zai immediately if either is falling behind. Four distinct pools are utilized to segregate funds: ○ 3 Member Pools: Individual holding accounts for contributions. ○ 1 Main Bills Pool: The central bucket from which all shared obligations are paid. Workﬂow 1. Weekly Funding: Funds are moved into individual pools throughout the week. 2. Weekly Settlement: Bills are paid from the Main Bills Pool at the end of each week.

# Page 2^page=2
3. Monthly Reconciliation: A formal snapshot and reconciliation are performed at month-end to prevent ledger bloat. Roles & Domain Authorities Responsibilities are divided based on domain expertise to ensure no single person carries the full administrative load: ● ( Mom) : Management of monthly housing bills and grocery procurement. ● ( Zai) : Maintenance of vehicles, pet care for Gigi, and related supplies. ● ( Cody) : System administration, IT infrastructure, legal oversight, and ﬁnancial bookkeeping. Shared Bill Ledger The following items are recognized as shared expenses and processed through the Bills Pool: Bill Name Amount Frequency Category Due 🏠 Rent $750.00 Monthly Housing 1st ⚡ Electric $150.00 Monthly Utilities 10th 💧 Water $100.00 Monthly Utilities 15th 🌐 Internet $45.00 Monthly Utilities 20th

# Page 3^page=3
Bill Name Amount Frequency Category Due 🛡 Insurance $20.00 Monthly Insurance 5th 🧼 Supplies $50.00 Monthly Household 25th 🐾 Pet Expenses $20.00 Monthly Pet N/A 💰 Savings $50.00 Monthly Savings N/A Total $1,185.00 Note: Variable costs (Gas, Trash, Sewer) are added only if billed separately from standard utilities. Operational Heuristics To ensure the system remains functional and low-stress, the following "Rules of Thumb" apply: 1. The $20/Day Rule The baseline contribution for the combined living-cost picture is approximately $14.00 to $20.00 a day to cover the household. This total is adjusted monthly based on actual utility ﬂuctuations. 2. Grocery "2-for-1" Equivalence For grocery splits involving EBT or speciﬁc dietary needs, an informal 2-for-1 rule of thumb is applied to maintain equivalence in splits without itemizing every receipt.

# Page 4^page=4
3. Simplicity over Precision ● Rounding: All transfers should be rounded to the nearest dollar where possible to simplify Cash App movements. ● Variable Buffering: Variable bills are reconciled monthly rather than debating individual transaction spikes. ● Documentation: Exceptions to these rules must be written down immediately; verbal debates from memory are discouraged. Reconciliation & Record Keeping At the end of each billing cycle, the household will perform a cleanup following standard bookkeeping principles: ● Verify all payments made against the payments ledger. ● Adjust share_amount_actual to reﬂect real-world utility costs. ● Review the "Bill Name" list to ensure all active services are captured. Agreement Effective Date: Date
